NKJV.1: Now concerning the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you;
NKJV.2: for I know your willingness, about which I boast of you to the Macedonians, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal has stirred up the majority.
NKJV.3: Yet I have sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this respect, that, as I said, you may be ready;
NKJV.4: lest if some Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we (not to mention you!) should be ashamed of this confident boasting.
NKJV.5: Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren to go to you ahead of time, and prepare your generous gift beforehand, which you had previously promised, that it may be ready as a matter of generosity and not as a grudging obligation.
NKJV.6: But this I say: He who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and he who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
NKJV.7: So let each one give as he purposes in his heart, not grudgingly or of necessity; for God loves a cheerful giver.
NKJV.8: And God is able to make all grace abound toward you, that you, always having all sufficiency in all things, may have an abundance for every good work.
NKJV.9: As it is written:  “He has dispersed abroad, He has given to the poor; His righteousness endures forever.”
NKJV.10: Now may He who supplies seed to the sower, and bread for food, supply and multiply the seed you have sown and increase the fruits of your righteousness,
NKJV.11: while you are enriched in everything for all liberality, which causes thanksgiving through us to God.
NKJV.12: For the administration of this service not only supplies the needs of the saints, but also is abounding through many thanksgivings to God,
NKJV.13: while, through the proof of this ministry, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal sharing with them and all men,
NKJV.14: and by their prayer for you, who long for you because of the exceeding grace of God in you.
NKJV.15: Thanks be to God for His indescribable gift!

ESV.1: Now it is superfluous for me to write to you about the ministry for the saints,
ESV.2: for I know your readiness, of which I boast about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year. And your zeal has stirred up most of them.
ESV.3: But I am sending  the brothers so that our boasting about you may not prove empty in this matter, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be.
ESV.4: Otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated — to say nothing of you — for being so confident.
ESV.5: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance for the gift  you have promised, so that it may be ready as a willing gift, not as an exaction.
ESV.6: The point is this: whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows bountifully  will also reap bountifully.
ESV.7: Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
ESV.8: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that having all sufficiency  in all things at all times, you may abound in every good work.
ESV.9: As it is written, “He has distributed freely, he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.”
ESV.10: He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
ESV.11: You will be enriched in every way to be generous in every way, which through us will produce thanksgiving to God.
ESV.12: For the ministry of this service is not only supplying the needs of the saints but is also overflowing in many thanksgivings to God.
ESV.13: By their approval of this service, they  will glorify God because of your submission that comes from your confession of the gospel of Christ, and the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others,
ESV.14: while they long for you and pray for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.
ESV.15: Thanks be to God for his inexpressible gift!

NASB.1: For it is superfluous for me to write to you about this ministry to the saints;
NASB.2: for I know your readiness, of which I boast about you to the Macedonians, [namely], that Achaia has been prepared since last year, and your zeal has stirred up most of them.
NASB.3: But I have sent the brethren, in order that our boasting about you may not be made empty in this case, so that, as I was saying, you may be prepared;
NASB.4: otherwise if any Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we-- not to speak of you-- will be put to shame by this confidence.
NASB.5: So I thought it necessary to urge the brethren that they would go on ahead to you and arrange beforehand your previously promised bountiful gift, so that the same would be ready as a bountiful gift and not affected by covetousness.
NASB.6: Now this [I say], he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and he who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
NASB.7: Each one [must do] just as he has purposed in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
NASB.8: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that always having all sufficiency in everything, you may have an abundance for every good deed;
NASB.9: as it is written, 'HE SCATTERED ABROAD, HE GAVE TO THE POOR, HIS RIGHTEOUSNESS ENDURES FOREVER.'
NASB.10: Now He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness;
NASB.11: you will be enriched in everything for all liberality, which through us is producing thanksgiving to God.
NASB.12: For the ministry of this service is not only fully supplying the needs of the saints, but is also overflowing through many thanksgivings to God.
NASB.13: Because of the proof given by this ministry, they will glorify God for [your] obedience to your confession of the gospel of Christ and for the liberality of your contribution to them and to all,
NASB.14: while they also, by prayer on your behalf, yearn for you because of the surpassing grace of God in you.
NASB.15: Thanks be to God for His indescribable gift!

NLT.1: I really don’t need to write to you about this ministry of giving for the believers in Jerusalem.
NLT.2: For I know how eager you are to help, and I have been boasting to the churches in Macedonia that you in Greece were ready to send an offering a year ago. In fact, it was your enthusiasm that stirred up many of the Macedonian believers to begin giving.
NLT.3: But I am sending these brothers to be sure you really are ready, as I have been telling them, and that your money is all collected. I don’t want to be wrong in my boasting about you.
NLT.4: We would be embarrassed — not to mention your own embarrassment — if some Macedonian believers came with me and found that you weren’t ready after all I had told them!
NLT.5: So I thought I should send these brothers ahead of me to make sure the gift you promised is ready. But I want it to be a willing gift, not one given grudgingly.
NLT.6: Remember this — a farmer who plants only a few seeds will get a small crop. But the one who plants generously will get a generous crop.
NLT.7: You must each decide in your heart how much to give. And don’t give reluctantly or in response to pressure. “For God loves a person who gives cheerfully.”
NLT.8: And God will generously provide all you need. Then you will always have everything you need and plenty left over to share with others.
NLT.9:
As the Scriptures say, “They share freely and give generously to the poor.
Their good deeds will be remembered forever.”
NLT.10: For God is the one who provides seed for the farmer and then bread to eat. In the same way, he will provide and increase your resources and then produce a great harvest of generosity in you.
NLT.11: Yes, you will be enriched in every way so that you can always be generous. And when we take your gifts to those who need them, they will thank God.
NLT.12: So two good things will result from this ministry of giving — the needs of the believers in Jerusalem will be met, and they will joyfully express their thanks to God.
NLT.13: As a result of your ministry, they will give glory to God. For your generosity to them and to all believers will prove that you are obedient to the Good News of Christ.
NLT.14: And they will pray for you with deep affection because of the overflowing grace God has given to you.
NLT.15: Thank God for this gift too wonderful for words!

WEB.1: It is indeed unnecessary for me to write to you concerning the service to the saints,
WEB.2: for I know your readiness, of which I boast on your behalf to them of Macedonia, that Achaia has been prepared for a year past. Your zeal has stirred up very many of them.
WEB.3: But I have sent the brothers that our boasting on your behalf may not be in vain in this respect, that, just as I said, you may be prepared,
WEB.4: so that I won’t by any means, if anyone from Macedonia comes there with me and finds you unprepared, we (to say nothing of you) should be disappointed in this confident boasting.
WEB.5: I thought it necessary therefore to entreat the brothers that they would go before to you, and arrange ahead of time the generous gift that you promised before, that the same might be ready as a matter of generosity, and not of greediness.
WEB.6: Remember this: he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly. He who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
WEB.7: Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart; not grudgingly, or under compulsion; for God loves a cheerful giver.
WEB.8: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that you, always having all sufficiency in everything, may abound to every good work.
WEB.9: As it is written, “He has scattered abroad, he has given to the poor. His righteousness remains forever.”
WEB.10: Now may he who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food, supply and multiply your seed for sowing, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;
WEB.11: you being enriched in everything to all liberality, which produces through us thanksgiving to God.
WEB.12: For this service of giving that you perform not only makes up for lack among the saints, but abounds also through many givings of thanks to God;
WEB.13: seeing that through the proof given by this service, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the Good News of Christ, and for the liberality of your contribution to them and to all;
WEB.14: while they themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, yearn for you by reason of the exceeding grace of God in you.
WEB.15: Now thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift!

CSB17.1: Now concerning the ministry to the saints, it is unnecessary for me to write to you.
CSB17.2: For I know your eagerness, and I boast about you to the Macedonians: <sup>ⓔ</sup> “Achaia <sup>ⓕ</sup> has been ready since last year,” and your zeal <sup>ⓖ</sup> has stirred up most of them. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.3: But I am sending the brothers so that our boasting about you in this matter would not prove empty, and so that you would be ready just as I said.
CSB17.4: Otherwise, if any Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we, not to mention you, would be put to shame in that situation. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.5: Therefore I considered it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance the generous gift <sup>ⓘ</sup> you promised, so that it will be ready as a gift and not as an extortion. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.6: The point is this: <sup>[2]</sup> The person who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the person who sows generously will also reap generously.
CSB17.7: Each person should do as he has decided in his heart — not reluctantly or out of compulsion, since God loves <sup>ⓚ</sup> a cheerful giver.
CSB17.8: And God is able <sup>ⓛ</sup> to make every grace overflow to you, so that in every way, always having everything you need, you may excel in every good work.
CSB17.9:
As it is written: He distributed freely; ⓜ
he gave to the poor;
his righteousness endures forever. [3] ⓝ
CSB17.10: Now the one who provides seed for the sower and bread for food will also provide and multiply your seed and increase the harvest of your righteousness. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.11: You will be enriched <sup>ⓑ</sup> in every way for all generosity, which produces thanksgiving to God through us.
CSB17.12: For the ministry of this service <sup>ⓒ</sup> is not only supplying the needs of the saints but is also overflowing in many expressions of thanks to God.
CSB17.13: Because of the proof provided by this ministry, they will glorify God for your obedient confession of the gospel of Christ, and for your generosity <sup>ⓓ</sup> in sharing with them and with everyone.
CSB17.14: And as they pray on your behalf, they will have deep affection for you because of the surpassing grace of God in you.
CSB17.15: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!

AMP.1: Now it is unnecessary for me to write to you about the offering [that is to be made] for the saints [in Jerusalem];
AMP.2: for I know your eagerness [to promote this cause], and I have [proudly] boasted to the people of Macedonia about it, telling them that Achaia has been prepared since last year [for this contribution], and your enthusiasm has inspired the majority of them [to respond].
AMP.3: Still, I am sending the brothers [on to you], so that our pride in you may not be an empty boast in this case, and so that you may be prepared, just as I told them you would be;
AMP.4: otherwise, if any Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we—to say nothing of yourselves—will be humiliated for being so confident.
AMP.5: That is why I thought it necessary to urge these brothers to go to you [before I come] and make arrangements in advance for this generous, previously promised gift of yours, so that it would be ready, not as something extorted [or wrung out of you], but as a [voluntary and] generous gift.
AMP.6: Now [remember] this: he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and he who sows generously [that blessings may come to others] will also reap generously [and be blessed].
AMP.7: Let each one give [thoughtfully and with purpose] just as he has decided in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver [and delights in the one whose heart is in his gift].
AMP.8: And God is able to make all grace [every favor and earthly blessing] come in abundance to you, so that you may always [under all circumstances, regardless of the need] have complete sufficiency in everything [being completely self-sufficient in Him], and have an abundance for every good work and act of charity.
AMP.9:
As it is written and forever remains written,
"He [the benevolent and generous person] scattered abroad, he gave to the poor,
His righteousness endures forever!"
AMP.10: Now He who provides seed for the sower and bread for food will provide and multiply your seed for sowing [that is, your resources] and increase the harvest of your righteousness [which shows itself in active goodness, kindness, and love].
AMP.11: You will be enriched in every way so that you may be generous, and this [generosity, administered] through us is producing thanksgiving to God [from those who benefit].
AMP.12: For the ministry of this service (offering) is not only supplying the needs of the saints (God's people), but is also overflowing through many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
AMP.13: Because of this act of ministry, they will glorify God for your obedience to the gospel of Christ which you confess, as well as for your generous participation [in this gift] for them and for all [the other believers in need],
AMP.14: and they also long for you while they pray on your behalf, because of the surpassing measure of God's grace [His undeserved favor, mercy, and blessing which is revealed] in you.
AMP.15: Now thanks be to God for His indescribable gift [which is precious beyond words]!

MSG.1: If I wrote any more on this relief offering for the poor Christians, I'd be repeating myself.
MSG.2: I know you're on board and ready to go. I've been bragging about you all through Macedonia province, telling them, "Achaia province has been ready to go on this since last year." Your enthusiasm by now has spread to most of them.
MSG.3: Now I'm sending the brothers to make sure you're ready, as I said you would be, so my bragging won't turn out to be just so much hot air.
MSG.4: If some Macedonians and I happened to drop in on you and found you weren't prepared, we'd all be pretty red-faced—you and us—for acting so sure of ourselves.
MSG.5: So to make sure there will be no slipup, I've recruited these brothers as an advance team to get you and your promised offering all ready before I get there. I want you to have all the time you need to make this offering in your own way. I don't want anything forced or hurried at the last minute.
MSG.6: Remember: A stingy planter gets a stingy crop; a lavish planter gets a lavish crop.
MSG.7: I want each of you to take plenty of time to think it over, and make up your own mind what you will give. That will protect you against sob stories and arm-twisting. God loves it when the giver delights in the giving.
MSG.8: God can pour on the blessings in astonishing ways so that you're ready for anything and everything, more than just ready to do what needs to be done.
MSG.9: As one psalmist puts it, He throws caution to the winds, giving to the needy in reckless abandon. His right-living, right-giving ways never run out, never wear out.
MSG.10: This most generous God who gives seed to the farmer that becomes bread for your meals is more than extravagant with you. He gives you something you can then give away, which grows into full-formed lives, robust in God,
MSG.11: wealthy in every way, so that you can be generous in every way, producing with us great praise to God.
MSG.12: Carrying out this social relief work involves far more than helping meet the bare needs of poor Christians. It also produces abundant and bountiful thanksgivings to God.
MSG.13: This relief offering is a prod to live at your very best, showing your gratitude to God by being openly obedient to the plain meaning of the Message of Christ. You show your gratitude through your generous offerings to your needy brothers and sisters, and really toward everyone.
MSG.14: Meanwhile, moved by the extravagance of God in your lives, they'll respond by praying for you in passionate intercession for whatever you need.
MSG.15: Thank God for this gift, his gift. No language can praise it enough!

NIV.1: There is no need for me to write to you about this service to the saints.
NIV.2: For I know your eagerness to help, and I have been boasting about it to the Macedonians, telling them that since last year you in Achaia were ready to give; and your enthusiasm has stirred most of them to action.
NIV.3: But I am sending the brothers in order that our boasting about you in this matter should not prove hollow, but that you may be ready, as I said you would be.
NIV.4: For if any Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we—not to say anything about you—would be ashamed of having been so confident.
NIV.5: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to visit you in advance and finish the arrangements for the generous gift you had promised. Then it will be ready as a generous gift, not as one grudgingly given.
NIV.6:
Sowing Generously
Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.
NIV.7: Each man should give what he has decided in his heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
NIV.8: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.
NIV.9:
As it is written:
“He has scattered abroad his gifts to the poor;
his righteousness endures forever.”
NIV.10: Now he who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will also supply and increase your store of seed and will enlarge the harvest of your righteousness.
NIV.11: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous on every occasion, and through us your generosity will result in thanksgiving to God.
NIV.12: This service that you perform is not only supplying the needs of God’s people but is also overflowing in many expressions of thanks to God.
NIV.13: Because of the service by which you have proved yourselves, men will praise God for the obedience that accompanies your confession of the gospel of Christ, and for your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone else.
NIV.14: And in their prayers for you their hearts will go out to you, because of the surpassing grace God has given you.
NIV.15: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!

NRSVCE.1: Now it is not necessary for me to write you about the ministry to the saints,
NRSVCE.2: for I know your eagerness, which is the subject of my boasting about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year; and your zeal has stirred up most of them.
NRSVCE.3: But I am sending the brothers in order that our boasting about you may not prove to have been empty in this case, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be;
NRSVCE.4: otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated — to say nothing of you — in this undertaking.
NRSVCE.5: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you, and arrange in advance for this bountiful gift that you have promised, so that it may be ready as a voluntary gift and not as an extortion.
NRSVCE.6: The point is this: the one who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the one who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
NRSVCE.7: Each of you must give as you have made up your mind, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
NRSVCE.8: And God is able to provide you with every blessing in abundance, so that by always having enough of everything, you may share abundantly in every good work.
NRSVCE.9:
As it is written, “He scatters abroad, he gives to the poor;
his righteousness endures forever.”
NRSVCE.10: He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
NRSVCE.11: You will be enriched in every way for your great generosity, which will produce thanksgiving to God through us;
NRSVCE.12: for the rendering of this ministry not only supplies the needs of the saints but also overflows with many thanksgivings to God.
NRSVCE.13: Through the testing of this ministry you glorify God by your obedience to the confession of the gospel of Christ and by the generosity of your sharing with them and with all others,
NRSVCE.14: while they long for you and pray for you because of the surpassing grace of God that he has given you.
NRSVCE.15: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!

NET.1: For it is not necessary for me to write you about this service to the saints,
NET.2: because I know your eagerness to help. I keep boasting to the Macedonians about this eagerness of yours, that Achaia has been ready to give since last year, and your zeal to participate has stirred up most of them.
NET.3: But I am sending these brothers so that our boasting about you may not be empty in this case, so that you may be ready just as I kept telling them.
NET.4: For if any of the Macedonians should come with me and find that you are not ready to give, we would be humiliated (not to mention you) by this confidence we had in you.
NET.5: Therefore I thought it necessary to urge these brothers to go to you in advance and to arrange ahead of time the generous contribution you had promised, so this may be ready as a generous gift and not as something you feel forced to do.
NET.6: My point is this: The person who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the person who sows generously will also reap generously.
NET.7: Each one of you should give just as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, because God loves a cheerful giver.
NET.8: And God is able to make all grace overflow to you so that because you have enough of everything in every way at all times, you will overflow in every good work.
NET.9: Just as it is written, “He has scattered widely, he has given to the poor; his righteousness remains forever.”
NET.10: Now God who provides seed for the sower and bread for food will provide and multiply your supply of seed and will cause the harvest of your righteousness to grow.
NET.11: You will be enriched in every way so that you may be generous on every occasion, which is producing through us thanksgiving to God,
NET.12: because the service of this ministry is not only providing for the needs of the saints but is also overflowing with many thanks to God.
NET.13: Through the evidence of this service they will glorify God because of your obedience to your confession in the gospel of Christ and the generosity of your sharing with them and with everyone.
NET.14: And in their prayers on your behalf they long for you because of the extraordinary grace God has shown to you.
NET.15: Thanks be to God for his indescribable gift!
